course_title: Teacher Autonomy, Professional Trust and Reflective Practice
downloadable_title: Ethical Use of Professional Freedom - Professional Downloadable Toolkit
core_terms: teacher autonomy, reflective judgement and collaborative professional learning
short_explanation: This downloadable toolkit supports Ethical Use of Professional Freedom within Teacher Autonomy, Professional Trust and Reflective Practice. It helps participants translate Finnish education principles into a reusable professional artefact, not simply complete a reading activity.
okm_framing: The resource connects classroom, leadership and institutional development to the wider Ministry of Education and Culture policy context: education improvement should be planned as part of system development, quality, inclusion, research-informed practice and public responsibility.
edufi_framing: The resource uses EDUFI-aligned wording by treating curriculum and learning design as a common foundation that supports equality while allowing local adaptation. Participants connect national-level principles to local curriculum, school culture, pedagogy and learner experience.
fineec_framing: The resource uses FINEEC-benchmarked wording by applying enhancement-led development: participation, trust, learning, appreciation, strengths, development targets and impact. Evidence is used to improve practice, not to blame individuals.

key_definitions.principle: the educational value or idea being made visible in practice
key_definitions.problem: the real learning, teaching, leadership, curriculum or institutional challenge being addressed
key_definitions.tool: the template, routine, planning document, dialogue structure or evidence instrument used to respond to the challenge
key_definitions.evidence: visible proof that the action has been applied, reviewed and connected to improvement
key_definitions.improvement_cycle: identify priority, plan action, implement, collect evidence, reflect, adjust and decide the next step
key_definitions.enhancement_led_development: a participatory, trust-based improvement approach focused on learning, strengths, development targets and impact

meaning_in_practice: In practice, participants use the toolkit to identify one priority related to Ethical Use of Professional Freedom, choose a manageable action, complete the professional template, collect evidence and decide what should improve next.
evidence_requirement: reflective practice log, peer dialogue note and professional decision record
model_example: A participant notices that ethical use of professional freedom is discussed in general terms but is not yet visible in daily practice. They complete the toolkit by selecting one priority, designing a focused action, collecting one piece of evidence, and writing a next-step improvement decision.
reflection_prompt: What changed in practice after using this Ethical Use of Professional Freedom resource? What evidence supports your judgement? What should be continued, adapted, scaled or redesigned?
certificate_rule: The downloadable evidence is complete when the participant submits a filled Ethical Use of Professional Freedom - Professional Downloadable Toolkit, includes evidence connected to reflective practice log, peer dialogue note and professional decision record, and writes one improvement decision aligned to the FCEI Finnish Teacher Training Development Framework.
